Docs sidebar

Continuity revalidation exception summary

Summary card that should be dropped.

Continuity revalidation exception matrix

Continuity revalidation exception matrices matter when operators need a precise map of which incident conditions justify bypassing the default revalidation flow.

The matrix ties exception paths to review checkpoints, queue health evidence, fallback queues, and incident severity rather than informal judgement.

Teams are expected to preserve fairness controls, handoff records, and recovery evidence so exception handling remains auditable across shifts.

Longer incidents still require explicit rollback ownership and a fresh approval check before an exception path is extended.
